text: They watched the new person take the seat at the table that had always been theirs, and when introductions were made they did not offer a hand, and their voice when they said hello did not match the word

She read the email from the committee chair twice, noting the way it was phrased to sound collaborative while assigning her every undesirable task, and she typed her reply with the delete key getting more use than any other

The neighbor's fence was exactly one inch over the property line and they had measured it four times with a tape measure and photographed each measurement and saved the photos in a folder labeled evidence
When the waiter brought the wrong dish for the third time he did not send it back, he ate it in silence, but he calculated the tip down to the cent and left it in coins
The brother-in-law made another comment about the size of the house and they smiled and said nothing and later that evening moved his luggage from the guest room to the smaller room near the laundry

They noticed the coworker had been cc'd on the email to the director, the email that contained their idea presented as a team effort, and they began composing a separate message with the original draft attached and timestamped
She held the door open for the man who had cut in line at the pharmacy and said after you with a brightness that made the pharmacist glance up from the counter

The reviewer had given the manuscript a single star and written two paragraphs explaining why, and the author read it once, bookmarked the reviewer's own published work, and began reading it with a pen in hand

Every time the upstairs tenant ran the washing machine at midnight the ceiling shook and the light fixture swayed and they lay in bed staring at it and adding another entry to the log they were keeping

The customer returned the item and demanded a refund in a voice that carried across the store, and the clerk processed it with a smile that never reached the eyes and a receipt that was handed over without a word

When the team lead asked for volunteers, everyone looked at the table, and when the lead assigned the task to them specifically they wrote the name down in the notebook next to the four other times this had happened
At the reunion the former classmate made a joke about their old stutter and the table laughed and they laughed too and then did not speak to that person again for the rest of the evening or ever
They parked their car precisely six inches from the line, perfectly legal, making it nearly impossible for the SUV that always took two spaces to open its driver-side door
The landlord's reply to the maintenance request was a single sentence about being patient and they printed it and taped it to the wall next to the spreading water stain

She listened to the man explain her own field of expertise to her at the dinner party, nodding at intervals, and when he finished she asked him a question so specific and technical that the table went quiet
The dog growled low and continuous at the visitor, not barking, not moving, just a vibration in its chest that did not stop even when the visitor sat down and tried to look comfortable
They returned the borrowed book with a list of its factual errors bookmarked with color-coded sticky notes and a handwritten bibliography of better sources on the same topic
When asked to share their screen in the meeting, they paused just long enough for the silence to become noticeable before clicking the button and saying of course, happy to
The chef tasted the dish the new hire had prepared and set the spoon down without expression and said try again and walked away without specifying what was wrong
They learned the HOA bylaws thoroughly, all forty-seven pages, and began attending every meeting with a printed copy and a highlighter, asking questions that required the board to look things up
The mother-in-law rearranged the kitchen cabinets while they were at work and when they came home everything was in the wrong place and she said she thought it made more sense this way
He answered every question in the interview with the minimum number of words necessary, maintaining eye contact a beat too long each time, and when they asked if he had questions he said no

The coach put the backup in for the final quarter and did not look at the starter on the bench and the starter sat very still with their hands between their knees and did not cheer when the team scored
She made the pie for the potluck using the exact recipe the other woman had claimed was a family secret, having found it in a twenty-year-old community cookbook at the library
The passenger reclined their seat all the way back on the red-eye and the person behind them placed their overhead light on and opened a bag of chips and ate them one at a time for an hour
When the new policy was announced the veteran employee said nothing in the meeting but afterwards sent a detailed email to every person on the team explaining exactly what the policy would cost them
The tennis player picked up the ball after the disputed call and held it for a long moment, looking at the line judge, before walking back to serve, and the next serve was the hardest of the match
They cleaned out the shared office refrigerator and threw away everything without labels including the lunch they knew belonged to the person who had reported them to HR

The older sibling corrected the younger one's pronunciation at Thanksgiving dinner, in front of the entire family, enunciating the word slowly as though teaching a child, though the younger one was thirty-four
She donated the painting her ex had given her to a thrift store in the town where his mother lived and made sure the donation receipt was emailed to the address they once shared
The committee member voted no on every proposal brought by the same colleague, regardless of the content, and kept a neutral expression throughout and cited different procedural reasons each time

He noticed the scratch on the car door the morning after the argument with the neighbor about the hedge, and he photographed it and said nothing and installed a camera facing the driveway that afternoon
The barista wrote the customer's name wrong for the fifth consecutive visit, always a different wrong spelling, and handed over the cup with the same cheerful here you go
When the promotion was announced and it was not them, they sent a congratulations email to the person who got it, two sentences long, period at the end of each, no exclamation points, no emojis
The guitarist turned up the amp one notch louder than necessary during the other guitarist's solo and looked straight ahead at the audience as though nothing had changed
They invited everyone from the office to the party except the one person, and when that person found out, they said it must have been an oversight, in a tone that communicated it had not been

The student handed in the assignment on the exact deadline, not a minute early, and it met every requirement precisely, nothing more and nothing less, formatted with mechanical perfection and devoid of any effort beyond the minimum
She set the thermostat to sixty-two degrees because her husband's mother was visiting and his mother had once mentioned preferring warm rooms and said nothing when the woman reached for a blanket
The child chose the other parent's hand to hold at the crosswalk and did not look back, and the other parent glanced over with an expression that was not quite a smile and not quite not one

When the opposing lawyer shook hands after the verdict the grip was firm and held a second too long and the words good game were spoken as though the courtroom had been an arena and the outcome a score
